purpose: Bind repo and terminal work to the real Codex command_exec lifecycle so exec-session continuity, reads, writes, and one-shot commands stay grounded in actual machine execution.

capabilities.goal: Use the real Codex command_exec engine correctly for one-shot shell work and persistent exec-session work without inventing session continuity or machine results.

oneShot: Use `execCommand` for a single non-interactive bounded shell step.
Keep every `execCommand` array item JSON-safe and single-line; raw literal newlines, heredocs, and embedded multi-line script bodies do not belong inside `execCommand`.
session: Use `execSessionAction=start` to open a persistent session.
Use `execSessionAction=write` to send bytes to stdin.
Use `execSessionAction=read` only to retrieve the buffered snapshot that already exists.
If the bounded shell work needs multiple lines or script content, default to `execSessionAction=start` plus `write` instead of forcing that content into `execCommand`.

successEvidence: If a session was claimed, the same turn actually returned `execSessionAction` and the machine path succeeded.
If `read` was used, the checkpoint detail reflects buffered stdout/stderr from the real session snapshot, not an imagined command result.
If `write` was used, the checkpoint detail identifies what input was sent and which session received it.
If `execCommand` was used, the checkpoint detail names the actual command, cwd, and result.
Control JSON stayed valid; machine work was not lost to malformed multi-line string output.
failureBoundaries: A prose claim that a session exists does not count as session continuity.
Repeated `read` on the same empty session snapshot is not progress.
If `read` stays empty, diagnose the prior `write`, newline, shell state, or missing output instead of rereading forever.
If a session id is missing or dead, either reuse a visible real session or start a new one.
If no machine path ran, the turn may only claim planning or inspection.
Malformed JSON caused by raw multi-line command strings is a contract failure; switch to exec-session writes or smaller one-line bounded commands.
neverDo: Never claim an exec session was opened unless the same turn actually returned `execSessionAction=start`.
Never treat `execSessionAction=read` as if it executed a command.
Never invent vanity session ids when no such real session is visible.
Never persist build, test, edit, or runtime claims without matching command_exec evidence.
Never loop on empty `read` output without an intervening state change.
Never place heredocs or raw multi-line script bodies directly into `execCommand` JSON strings.
